required_skills: Payload system architecture design for electro-optical (EO), infrared (IR), SAR, or RF intelligence payloads
Optical system design fundamentals including aperture sizing, focal length, MTF, and signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) analysis
Payload performance characterisation including ground sampling distance (GSD), swath width, and radiometric sensitivity
Detector and focal plane array (FPA) selection and characterisation for imaging and sensing payloads
Payload-to-spacecraft interface management covering mechanical, thermal, power, and data interfaces
Payload thermal control and dissipation design for cryogenic and ambient-temperature sensing systems
Payload data handling, compression algorithms, and onboard processing architecture design
Calibration and characterisation methodology for space-borne remote sensing instruments
Electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) and RF interference management for payload integration
Payload environmental qualification testing per NASA GEVS, ECSS-E-ST-10, or MIL-STD-1540
preferred_skills: Experience with synthetic aperture radar (SAR) payload design, waveform generation, and image formation processing
Knowledge of hyperspectral or multispectral imaging system design and atmospheric correction algorithms
Familiarity with signals intelligence (SIGINT) or electronic intelligence (ELINT) payload architectures
Experience with laser ranging, LIDAR, or active remote sensing payload development
Knowledge of radiation effects on focal plane arrays and detector shielding strategies
Familiarity with real-time onboard data processing using FPGAs or GPUs for payload compression
Experience with payload ground segment design including image processing chains and data exploitation tools
responsibilities: Define payload system architecture and performance requirements by translating mission objectives into optical, RF, or SAR instrument specifications including GSD, SNR, swath, and radiometric resolution
Design and analyse electro-optical and infrared imaging systems using Zemax OpticStudio, performing modulation transfer function (MTF) and noise equivalent temperature difference (NETD) analysis
Manage payload-to-spacecraft interface definitions including mechanical mounting interfaces, thermal dissipation paths, power draw profiles, and high-speed data downlink allocations
Develop payload calibration and characterisation plans including pre-launch radiometric calibration, spectral calibration, and on-orbit vicarious calibration methodology
Execute payload thermal analysis using Thermal Desktop to verify detector and optical bench temperatures remain within operational and survival limits across the orbital thermal cycle
Lead payload integration and test activities in cleanroom environments, executing functional verification tests, optical alignment checks, and end-to-end performance demonstrations
Perform payload environmental qualification testing including vibration, acoustic, thermal vacuum (TVAC), and EMC tests to verify instrument robustness for the launch and space environments
Collaborate with mission operations and ground segment teams to define payload commanding modes, data product formats, and onboard scheduling logic for autonomous observation campaigns
Conduct trade studies on detector technology, optical material selection, and data compression schemes to optimise payload mass, power, and performance within spacecraft resource budgets
Author payload specification documents, interface control documents (ICDs), calibration data packages, and instrument user manuals to support programme reviews and customer deliverables
